text.title: Kubernetes Load Balancing: Services, Ingress, Probes Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Service type MUST match scope: ClusterIP for intra-cluster, NodePort for raw-host access, LoadBalancer only when cloud LB integration is intentional.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Every Ingress MUST set `ingressClassName`; reliance on the default class is forbidden in multi-controller clusters.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Every pod-backing Service MUST define a readiness probe; the absence of one causes traffic to hit not-ready pods during rolling updates.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Deployments with active connections MUST set `lifecycle.preStop` + `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` ≥30s to drain traffic.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: TLS certificates MUST be issued by cert-manager (or equivalent operator), not manually mounted secrets.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when no Service is in scope OR external LB fully handles the LB role with no Ingress.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
